innerBlocks.reason: Block does not use inner blocks

issues.description: ArrowLeft and ArrowRight SVG imports are unused in the TSX component. They are imported but never referenced in the JSX. The PHP template uses theme_block_asset() to render them instead.
issues.fix: Remove the unused imports: `import { ReactComponent as ArrowLeft } from './resources/ArrowLeft.svg';` and `import { ReactComponent as ArrowRight } from './resources/ArrowRight.svg';`

issues.description: Prettier formatting violations: the setAttributes type parameter (line 50) and aria-label i18n call (line 268) don't match expected formatting.
issues.fix: Run `bun run lint:fix` to auto-fix prettier formatting issues.

issues.description: BaseControl component has a label prop but no id prop at line 156. This creates an accessibility issue where the label is not associated with a form control.
issues.fix: Add an `id` prop to the BaseControl: `<BaseControl label={__('Selected Testimonials', 'takt')} id="testimonials-selector" __nextHasNoMarginBottom>`

issues.description: Video modal is rendered inside each block instance's PHP template (inside the hideIfEmpty conditional). With 6 block instances on the test page, 6 identical modals are rendered. The modal should be rendered once globally, not per block instance. The JS uses document.querySelector (first match only), so only the first modal is ever used -- the other 5 are dead DOM. While not functionally broken on the current page, this wastes DOM, and if a block with hideIfEmpty=true and no posts appears first, its modal won't render and subsequent blocks' video buttons would have no modal to open.
issues.fix: Move the video modal outside the block template. Use a wp_footer action hook to render it once, or use a static flag: `static $modal_rendered = false; if (!$modal_rendered) { $modal_rendered = true; /* render modal */ }`. Place this after the main endif.
issues.alsoUpdate: blocks/TestimonialsCarousel/TestimonialsCarousel.js:98 - JS already uses querySelector (first match), so no JS changes needed if modal is rendered once.

issues.description: The PHP swiper-parent div has class 'relative' which is missing from the TSX FilteredServerSideRender wrapper. PHP: `class="swiper-parent overflow-hidden grid grid-cols-1 grid-rows-1 relative"`. TSX: `className="swiper-parent overflow-hidden grid grid-cols-1 grid-rows-1"`. The 'relative' class is needed because the fade mask divs (testimonials-fade-left/right) use absolute positioning and require a positioned parent. In the editor, since FilteredServerSideRender extracts the PHP content and the wrapper gets the TSX classes, the fade masks may not position correctly in the editor preview.
issues.fix: Add 'relative' to the FilteredServerSideRender className: `className="swiper-parent overflow-hidden grid grid-cols-1 grid-rows-1 relative"`

issues.description: The video modal uses opacity/pointer-events toggling for show/hide instead of the native <dialog> element or inert attribute. When the modal is 'hidden' (opacity-0 pointer-events-none), screen readers may still announce the modal content since it remains in the DOM and is not hidden from the accessibility tree. The modal should use aria-hidden='true' when closed, or use the native <dialog> element.
issues.fix: Add aria-hidden='true' to the modal div by default. In the JS, toggle aria-hidden when opening/closing: `modal.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false')` in openModal() and `modal.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')` in closeModal().

issues.description: The video modal's openModal function constructs iframe/video innerHTML from string interpolation with the videoUrl parameter. The videoUrl comes from data attributes on buttons (data-video-url), which originate from post meta. While the PHP escapes with esc_attr(), the JS constructs innerHTML without additional sanitization. A malicious videoId meta value could inject HTML/JS via the iframe src. This is a stored XSS risk if testimonial posts can be created by untrusted users.
issues.fix: Use DOM API instead of innerHTML for creating the video element: `const video = document.createElement('video'); video.src = videoUrl; ...` or validate/sanitize the URL in JS before insertion. Alternatively, since only admins can create testimonials, document this as accepted risk.

issues.description: The experimental API warnings for __experimentalToggleGroupControl and __experimentalToggleGroupControlOption are consistent with other blocks in the codebase (DynamicContentCarousel, MasonryCards). These are standard WordPress experimental APIs used project-wide.
issues.fix: No action needed. Consistent with project convention.
